title: Meeting Probability

source.file: content/A Practical Guide To Quantitative Finance Interviews Book by Xinfeng Zhou (chapters)/chapter-04-probability-theory.md

setup.narrative: Two bankers each arrive at the office at a uniformly random time between 5:00 and 6:00 AM. Each stays for exactly 5 minutes after arriving, then leaves.
rules: Both arrival times X, Y are independent and uniform on [0, 60] (minutes after 5:00).
They MEET iff their visit intervals overlap, i.e., |X - Y| < 5.
setup.question: What is the probability they meet?

learningObjectives: Recognize the joint (X, Y) distribution as uniform on the [0, 60] x [0, 60] square.
Identify the favorable region |X - Y| < 5 as a band around the diagonal.
Compute the area of the band (via complement: two corner triangles).

stages.label: Identify the joint distribution
stages.prompt: X and Y are independent uniform on [0, 60]. What is the joint distribution of (X, Y)?

options.text: Uniform on the 60 x 60 square -- joint pdf = 1/3600 on the square, 0 elsewhere.

stages.explanation: Independent uniforms give joint uniform on the product space. Total area = 60 * 60 = 3600.

stages.label: Identify the favorable region
stages.prompt: They meet iff |X - Y| < 5. This is the region between the lines Y = X - 5 and Y = X + 5 inside the square. The COMPLEMENT region (no meeting) consists of:

options.text: Two right triangles in the corners (top-left and bottom-right), each with legs of length 60 - 5 = 55.

stages.explanation: The band |X - Y| < 5 sits along the diagonal. The two regions outside it are right triangles in opposite corners. Each has legs of 55 (60 - 5).

stages.label: Compute area of no-meeting region
stages.prompt: Sum of two right triangles, each with legs 55. Total area = 2 * (1/2 * 55 * 55) = ?

stages.label: Reflection
prompts: Generalize: each banker stays w minutes within a window of length T. P(meet)?
Verify: as w -> T, P(meet) -> 1. As w -> 0, P(meet) -> 0.
What if the bankers have DIFFERENT stay times w_1, w_2? Re-derive.
